text: from torch.utils.data import TensorDataset, DataLoader from torch.optim.lr_scheduler import StepLR
# define input size, hidden layer size, output size D_i, D_k, D_o = 10, 40, 5
# create model with two hidden layers model = nn.Sequential( nn.Linear(D_i, D_k), nn.ReLU(), nn.Linear(D_k, D_o), nn.ReLU(), nn.Linear(D_k, D_o) )
# He initialization of weights def weights_init(layer_in): if isinstance(layer_in, nn.Linear): nn.init.kaiming_normal_(layer_in.weight) layer_in.bias.data.fill(0.0)

# Choose least squares loss function criterion = nn.MSELoss() # construct SGD optimizer and initialize learning rate and momentum optimizer = torch.optim.SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.1, momentum=0.9) # object that decreases learning rate by half every 10 epochs scheduler = StepLR(optimizer, step_size=10, gamma=0.5)
# create 100 random data points and store in data loader class x = torch.rand(100, D_i) y = torch.randn(100, D_o)
# loop over batches for i, data in enumerate(data_loader): # retrieve inputs and labels for this batch x_batch, y_batch = data
# print error pred = model(x_batch) loss = criterion(pred, y_batch)
# = loss += loss.item() print(f'Epoch {epoch:5d}, loss {epoch_loss:.3f}') # tell scheduler to consider updating learning rate scheduler.step()
Figure 7.8 Sample code for training two-layer network on random data.
